title: Baystate Health doctor on probation after lewd behavior inside his office
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Dr. Robert A. Martin was placed on five-year probation after being caught masturbating in his office at Baystate Health.
The incident, observed by 10 to 20 employees, occurred on Nov. 21, 2023, at the Baystate Medical Center in Springfield.
The Massachusetts medical board stayed Martin's license suspension after he agreed to probation and behavioral health monitoring.

title: A brilliant example of biophilic design lists on Martha’s Vineyard for $22,950,000
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Minawetu, a $22,950,000 oceanfront property on Martha's Vineyard, utilizes biophilic design to connect with nature.
The 8,200-square-foot home features five en-suite bedrooms, a wine cellar, media room, gym, and a rooftop pool.
The property includes a detached building with two bedrooms, solar panels, and nearly 250 feet of sandy beach access.

title: Woman injured in Natick during marketplace meet-up to sell $27,000 diamond
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Keyjuan Webb, 23, allegedly attempted to steal a $27,000 diamond at Natick Mall, resulting in an altercation.
Seller Kathleen Sanfacon was injured while trying to stop Webb; a bystander was also bitten during the incident.
Webb faces multiple charges, including unarmed robbery; he is held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing.

title: He’s running Boston to support a family friend’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Sean Pruneau is running the Boston Marathon with Dana-Farber's Team Dennis to honor family friend Dennis Ford.
Dennis Ford was diagnosed with glioblastoma in 2023 and has raised over $2.5 million for the Jimmy Fund.
Team Dennis emphasizes 'Fueled by Positive Energy' to support efforts towards achieving a world without cancer.

title: Boston hotels increasing pricing, programming for the World Cup this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston hotels increase prices by over 300% for World Cup events, with Liberty Hotel rates exceeding $800 per night.
Seven World Cup games will be held at Gillette Stadium, rebranded as Boston Stadium, drawing over 2 million visitors.
Boston hotels offer special World Cup packages, including exclusive deals from XV Beacon, Hotel AKA, and The Dagny Boston.

title: ‘Violent fugitive’ wanted in Lawrence murder arrested in Maine
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jean Michael Warner Delgado, 20, wanted for a September homicide in Lawrence, was arrested in Baldwin, Maine.
Delgado was found during a welfare check in a vehicle on Bridgton Road, according to Maine authorities.
He is charged with murder and firearm possession, awaiting extradition to Massachusetts for arraignment.

title: Jayson Tatum will miss Celtics game against Thunder
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum will miss the Celtics game against the Thunder to manage a right Achilles injury.
Tatum has played on a minutes restriction since returning, averaging 19.7 points on 39.3% shooting.
The Celtics, second in the Eastern Conference, face NBA leaders Thunder in the last game of their road trip.

title: Boston City Council moves toward potential kratom ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council considers kratom ban due to health risks and its opioid-mimicking effects.
Officials and experts express concern over 7-OH, a compound in kratom, and its synthetic forms.
Councilor Ed Flynn and others push for ordinance, citing support from several Massachusetts towns.

title: ‘I was overcome’: Conan O’Brien tells Jimmy Kimmel about ’emotional’ trip to Ireland
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien visited his great-grandfather's former home in Galbally, Ireland, describing the trip as 'emotional' and 'powerful'.
O'Brien reflected on his great-grandfather's immigration journey as a tenant farmer, gaining empathy for immigrants' experiences.
O'Brien appeared on 'Jimmy Kimmel Live!' discussing his HBO Max show and the insights gained from his Ireland visit.

title: MBTA bus crashes into parked cars, home in Medford
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus crashed into parked cars and a home on Playstead Road in Medford early Sunday morning.
The bus swerved off the street at 1:44 a.m., hitting two vehicles that then struck a home's front steps.
MBTA Transit Police are investigating the incident; the driver cited black ice as the cause of losing control.
